content:
Hi,
I am trying to do Linux Rust build on Ubuntu 24.04 on x86 architecture. I am having some problems with installing the Rust environment. I set up on the rust-next branch of Linux-Rust. I followed the commands in https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust-next/Documentation/rust/quick-start.rst. I perform the following command

sudo apt install rustc-1.80 rust-1.80-src bindgen-0.65 rustfmt-1.80 rust-1.80-clippy
sudo apt install libncurses-dev gawk flex bison openssl libssl-dev dkms libelf-dev libudev-dev libpci-dev libiberty-dev autoconf llvm lld
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux.git -b rust-next
cd linux-rust
make LLVM=1 rustavailable
make LLVM=1 x86_64_rust_defconfig
make LLVM=1 -j $(nproc)

I created the following defconfig to active rust: x86_64_rust_defconfig

The problem is that I am having problems with version of rustc. Can any of you write down the commands you used to configure the environment and do the build?
what's the error?

I had this issue and talked with Miguel, I solved by tracking this:
“Please check the HAVE_RUST symbol then, i.e. follow the dependencies to see what is not met -- you are likely missing something in RUSTC_SUPPORTS_ARM64.”
And I was missing, don’t remember what, but it was a dependency that needed to be set or unset.
You can check the dependencies by writing / in menuconfig to search and search for RUST. And it is going to shown under the dependencies

Marcelo Borges ha scritto:

I had this issue and talked with Miguel, I solved by tracking this:
“Please check the HAVE_RUST symbol then, i.e. follow the dependencies to see what is not met -- you are likely missing something in RUSTC_SUPPORTS_ARM64.”
And I was missing, don’t remember what, but it was a dependency that needed to be set or unset.
You can check the dependencies by writing / in menuconfig to search and search for RUST. And it is going to shown under the dependencies

@Marcelo Borges please can you show me the command that you used for build?

@Ariel Miculas after apply the command

sudo apt install rustc-1.80 rust-1.80-src bindgen-0.65 rustfmt-1.80 rust-1.80-clippy
sudo apt install libncurses-dev gawk flex bison openssl libssl-dev dkms libelf-dev libudev-dev libpci-dev libiberty-dev autoconf llvm lld

I have the following error:

dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ make LLVM=1 rustavailable
make[1]: clang: No such file or directory
***
*** Rust bindings generator 'bindgen' could not be found.
***
***
*** Please see Documentation/rust/quick-start.rst for details
*** on how to set up the Rust support.
***
make[1]: *** [/home/dooraim/linux-rust-next/Makefile:1736: rustavailable] Error 1
make: *** [Makefile:224: __sub-make] Error 2

To solve this I apply the following command

dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ sudo update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/bindgen bindgen /usr/bin/bindgen-0.65 100
update-alternatives: using /usr/bin/bindgen-0.65 to provide /usr/bin/bindgen (bindgen) in auto mode

But have this error

dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ make LLVM=1 rustavailable
make[1]: clang: No such file or directory
***
*** Rust compiler 'rustc' is too old.
***   Your version:    1.75.0
***   Minimum version: 1.78.0
***
***
*** Please see Documentation/rust/quick-start.rst for details
*** on how to set up the Rust support.
***
make[1]: *** [/home/dooraim/linux-rust-next/Makefile:1736: rustavailable] Error 1
make: *** [Makefile:224: __sub-make] Error 2
dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$

To fix this I try 

dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ sudo update-alternatives --remove-all rustc
dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ sudo update-alternatives --install /usr/bin/rustc rustc /usr/bin/rustc-1.80 100
update-alternatives: using /usr/bin/rustc-1.80 to provide /usr/bin/rustc (rustc) in auto mode
update-alternatives: warning: not replacing /usr/bin/rustc with a link

If I used rustup have some problems with bindgen as show below

dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ sudo apt install rustup
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree... Done
Reading state information... Done
The following packages were automatically installed and are no longer required:
  libstd-rust-1.75 libstd-rust-dev
Use 'sudo apt autoremove' to remove them.
The following packages will be REMOVED:
  bindgen-0.65 rustc
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  rustup
0 upgraded, 1 newly installed, 2 to remove and 37 not upgraded.
Need to get 2,227 kB of archives.
After this operation, 9,022 kB disk space will be freed.
Do you want to continue? [Y/n] ^C

But have the error above. Some ideas?

Use the following distro version

dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ cat /etc/os-release
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 24.04.1 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="24.04"
VERSION="24.04.1 LTS (Noble Numbat)"
VERSION_CODENAME=noble
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
UBUNTU_CODENAME=noble
LOGO=ubuntu-logo
you can do sudo apt autoremove and try installing rustup again

Ariel Miculas

you can do sudo apt autoremove and try installing rustup again

rustup is not compatible with bindgen-0.65 but with bindgen-0.66.1 that is not compatible with rust-next of linux-rust. If you have time try it yourself, or tell me how you set up the build environment
uninstall bindgen and reinstall it using rustup
cargo install --locked bindgen-cli
unfortunately I'm running jammy so I can't test this quickly

Dooraim said:

or tell me how you set up the build environment

I did it recently on Ubuntu 22.04 "manually". I.e. not with the Ubuntu package repository via sudo apt ... but like described in the Quick start. I can't promise that this is complete and correct, but fyi some steps from the history:

sudo apt-get install clang clang-tools llvm lld
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
rustup override set $(scripts/min-tool-version.sh rustc)
rustup component add rust-src
cargo install --locked --version $(scripts/min-tool-version.sh bindgen) bindgen
cargo install bindgen-cli

If I read better https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust-next/Documentation/rust/quick-start.rst, after performing the following command, the doc says that we need to run also this command:

RUST_LIB_SRC=/usr/src/rustc-$(rustc --version | cut -d' ' -f2)/library

I don't run this command, maybe this can be the error.

It seems strange to me that the documentation at https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/blob/rust-next/Documentation/rust/quick-start.rst doesn't describe how to set up Ubuntu env correctly. It's more likely that I'm doing something wrong, but I don't understand it.

Dirk Behme ha scritto:

Dooraim said:

or tell me how you set up the build environment

I did it recently on Ubuntu 22.04 "manually". I.e. not with the Ubuntu package repository via sudo apt ... but like described in the Quick start. I can't promise that this is complete and correct, but fyi some steps from the history:

sudo apt-get install clang clang-tools llvm lld
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
rustup override set $(scripts/min-tool-version.sh rustc)
rustup component add rust-src
cargo install --locked --version $(scripts/min-tool-version.sh bindgen) bindgen
cargo install bindgen-cli

In case of Ubuntu-22.04 I used the same command and work correctly :wink:

Dooraim said:

In case of Ubuntu-22.04 I used the same command and work correctly :wink:

Uh, you want to say that it works on 22.04 but not on 24.04?

make[1]: clang: No such file or directory

Please note that you have errors about clang not being found. Can you build a non-Rust-enabled LLVM=1 kernel?

I just tried in a fresh Ubuntu 24.04, and I could build the kernel.

Please note that you need to setup first LLVM (including Clang) properly, and then, to enable Rust, you need to make sure you are using the versioned packages from Ubuntu (e.g. pass Make BINDGEN=bindgen-0.65 RUSTC=rustc-1.80 etc.) and finally check that your dependencies are met (e.g. with Ubuntu's Rust 1.80, you will need to take care of disabling CONFIG_CALL_PADDING if you were trying to use a defconfig, so it may be easier to just use rustup to get at least Rust 1.81).

Dirk Behme ha scritto:

Dooraim said:

In case of Ubuntu-22.04 I used the same command and work correctly :wink:

Uh, you want to say that it works on 22.04 but not on 24.04?

In Ubuntu 22.04 the commands work and I can build and install Linux-Rust but on Ubuntu 24.04 it doesn't

Miguel Ojeda ha scritto:

make[1]: clang: No such file or directory

Please note that you have errors about clang not being found. Can you build a non-Rust-enabled LLVM=1 kernel?

I fix it

I just tried in a fresh Ubuntu 24.04, and I could build the kernel.

Please note that you need to setup first LLVM (including Clang) properly, and then, to enable Rust, you need to make sure you are using the versioned packages from Ubuntu (e.g. pass Make BINDGEN=bindgen-0.65 RUSTC=rustc-1.80 etc.) and finally check that your dependencies are met (e.g. with Ubuntu's Rust 1.80, you will need to take care of disabling CONFIG_CALL_PADDING if you were trying to use a defconfig, so it may be easier to just use rustup to get at least Rust 1.81).

Ok follow your suggestion I have the following result

dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ make LLVM=1 RUSTC=rustc-1.80 BINDGEN=bindgen-0.65 RUST_LIB_SRC=/usr/src/rustc-1.80.1/library rustavailable
Rust is available!
dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ make LLVM=1 RUSTC=rustc-1.80 BINDGEN=bindgen-0.65 RUST_LIB_SRC=/usr/src/rustc-1.80.1/library defconfig
#
# No change to .config
#

active rust and run build

dooraim@noble:~/linux-rust-next$ time make -j${nproc} LLVM=1 RUSTC=rustc-1.80 BINDGEN=bindgen-0.65 RUST_LIB_SRC=/usr/src/rustc-1.80.1/library
...

Have some errors during build. In this case build was done on arm64
I don't know if it's normal to have errors during the build? Did it happen to you?
No it's not normal to have errors during the build.

I did more tests but with Ubuntu 24.04 I can't use it properly, I think the problem is related to the VM I use. I have a MacBook Pro with Apple Silicon M1. I then did a test with Arch Linux and had no build problem. Having said that I close the support request, if you need any info feel free to write me
Dooraim has marked this topic as resolved.
